California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
May 21", 1990
Cheryl A. Lara
Administrative Ass istant
Business Services and Self-Insured prograns
Madera County Superintendent of Schools
28l-23 Avenue L4
Madera, CA 93638
Re: Your Request for Informal, Assistance
our FiLe No. I-90-277
Dear Ms. Lara:
This letter is in response to your request for advj-ce regard-
ing the-discl-osure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the
"Act'1.1 
You seek our advice in order to assess the duties and
respons ibi l ities of attorneys $rho render legat services to the
office of the Madera Superintendent of Schools. your 1etter does
not state that you have been authorized by the parties to seek our
advice, nor have you provided sufficient facts for us to determine
the responsibil ities of the parties. 
Accordingly, we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to ReguLation
18329 (c) (copy enclosed. ) 2
OUESTION
Are the financial disclosure provisions of the Act applicable
to attorneys who contract with a public agency to provide legal
services?
CONCLUSION
The financial disclosure provisions of the Act may be ap-
plicable to attorneys who contract with a public agency to provide
1- Government Code Sections 81000-91015. A1l- statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. comnission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et secr. All references to regulations are to Tit1e 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not
inmunity provided by an opinion
tion 83114 i Regulation 18329(c)
provide the requestor with the
or formal written advice. (sec-
)
428 J Strcct, Suite 8flo a P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958o+-t)Srr 7 . (916) -lll-566rr


File No. l-9O-277
Page 2
legal services if the attorneys provide on-going legal advice to
the pubLic agency.
FACTS
Your office acts as the secretary/treasurer for three self
insured groups. Attorneys with the firm of Chinello, Chinello,
Shelton and Auchard perform legal. services for one of these
groups, the Central California School-s Risk l{anagement authority,
a public entity. 
These attorneys provide financial advice on
settlement of clairns and handle litigation.3
It appears that you recently forwarded a Form 730, Statement
of Economic Interests for Designated Empl,oyees to the Iaw firrn of
Chinello, Chinel1o, SheLton and Auchard and requested the law firm
to complete the forrn.l ttr. PauI Auchard, a ne;ber of the lae/
firm, wrote a letter to the Madera County Superintendent of
Schools dated March L3, L99O, stating that because the la!, firm is
not an employee of the Madera County Superintendent of Schools he
questioned erhether Form 730 was applicable to the firm. 
Mr.
Auchard further requested inforrnation such as 1egal citations and
written opinions concerning the applicability of Form 730 to
independent 1aw firms. 
You have for$rarded litr. Auchard,s letter to
us and requested our response to his inquiry.
ANALYSI S
Section 871O0 prohibits a publ,ic officiat 
from making or
participating in a governmental decision in which he or she knows
or has reason to know he or she has a financial interest. 
The
ten0 t'public officialtr includes any trmember, officer, 
employee or
consultantrr of a state or local government agency. (Section
82048, emphasis added. )
The Commission has def ined the tern rrconsuLtanttr as f oIlor.rs:
Itconsultanttr shall include any natural person
who provides, under contract, information, advice,
recommendation or counsel to a state or local
government agency, provided, however, that
rrconsultantrr sha1l not include a person who:
3 You provided this information during our tetephone conversation
of April 30, 1990.
4 i{e glean these facts from Mr. Auchard's letter of March 13,
1990.


Fi■e No. ■-90-277
Page 3
(A) Conducts research and arrives at
conclusions with respect to his or her rendi-
tion of information, advice, recommendation or
counsel independent of the control and direc-
tion of the agency or of any agency official,
other than normal contract monitoring; and
(B) Possesses no authority with respect
to any agency decision beyond the rendition of
information, advice, reconmendation or
counsel.
Regulation l-8700(a) (2) (A) and (B), copy
enclosed .
A consultant is a rrnatural person.rr Therefore, a 1aw firm is
not a consultant. 
Employees of a law firm, however, may be
consultants under the Act when they perform certain legaI services
under contract rrith a public agency.
You have advised me that the Iar., firrn provides financial
advice on the settlement of claims and handles titigation 
for the
Central California Schools Risk Management Authority. 
In general,
when a law firrn is retained to provide legal services for a
specific matter such as drafting a contract or handling a Iaw
suit, the attorneys providing those legal services are not
rrconsul-tantstt subject to the discl,osure provisions of the Act.
They possess no authority with respect to agency decisions beyond
the rendition of advice and recomnendations on the particular nat-
ter for vJhich they have been retained. The attorneys sinply
provide their lega1 expertise in the matter for which they have
been retained and submit the finished product to the agency. Thus,
they fall under the exception in Regulation 18700(a) (z)r EIIDEIL r
and are not consultants subject to the disclosure provisions of
the Act. 
(See Albucruerque Advice Letter, No. A-85-244, copy
encLosed. )
Conversely, if the services of an attorney are retained for
the purpose of providing on-going lega1 advice to the public
agency, such an attorney wouLd be a |tconsultant[ and subject to
the disclosure provisions of the Act. This is so because the at-
torney would be acting as a quasi-employee of the public agency
and would be participating in the decision-making process. 
Under
these circumstances, the attorney should be designated as a
consultant in the agency,s conf I ict-of- interest code and disclose
his or her financial interests in Forrn ?30, Statenent of Economic
Interests.


Fl■e No. I-90-277
Page 4
Based on the information you have provided to us, lre are un-
able to determine whether attorneys with the law firm of ChineJ-Io,
Chinello, shelton and Auchard are rrconsultantstt under the Act. I
trust this letter and the enclosed materials will enable you to
make this determination. Should you require further assistance in
this natter, do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5gOL.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral CounseL
ー
By:  B■anca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division
SH:BMB:p■h
Enclosures
